meta.tagline: Coney Island inpatient detox · South Brooklyn catchment
meta.author: Aleksandar Spasevski
meta.doc_subtitle: Map-pack dominant on the alcohol-rehab anchor (1.47 / 96% top-3) · service pages orphaned (104 sess / 0 conv / 365d) · 756 URLs not indexed · disavow + service-page rebuild + GBP depth fix + locations × services interlinking.

events_excluded.reason: Tracked as event in GA4 but not flagged as conversion. Per the standard HQDM lead composite (form_submit + click_phone_number + gmb_click + contact_form_submit), this should be flagged. Pending GA4 admin change.
Fires on the site but not marked as conversion. Likely a real form variant whose submissions are not being counted. Pending GA4 admin change.

anomalies: form_submit fires 237× / 90d but only 58 are flagged as conversion (~24%). Likely a config gap on one form variant or a conversion-flag toggle mid-window. Worth a GA4 admin audit before next quarterly review.

gbp_segment.method: GA4 custom segment filtered by sessionSource containing 'gmb'.
gbp_segment.channel_attribution_note: The GBP listing URL carries `?utm_source=gmb&utm_medium=click&utm_campaign=gmb_listing`. The `utm_medium=click` value is not in GA4's default channel-grouping rule set (which recognizes `organic`, `referral`, `cpc`/`ppc`, etc.) — so these sessions land in Direct or Unassigned by default. The 'subset of Organic Search' framing used in prior drafts referred to the custom segment, not the default channel; clarified here.
gbp_segment.fix_needed: Change `utm_medium=click` to `utm_medium=organic` on the GBP listing URL so the segment lands in Organic Search by default channel grouping. Vertical peers (LSA Recovery, Mountainside) use this pattern. Detail in Surfpoint_GBP_Audit_2026-05-12.md §3.5.

sources: data/ga4_trend.json — raw GA4 export (sessions, conversions, events by channel)

tldr: Surfpoint is overperforming its architecture. The GBP listing is the strongest single asset (rank 1.47 / 96% top-3 on the tracked alcohol-rehab anchor, improving Oct 2025 → Apr 2026; rank 1 across 9 of 12 priority service anchors at Coney Island center; 4.8★ / 120 reviews / +29 in 16 days; converts at 2.53% — 8× the all-Organic-Search rate of 0.32%). Organically, the site is being actively de-indexed by Google: 756 URLs flagged Crawled/Discovered — not indexed across 87 days; daily impressions −79% peak-to-current; 47 of 99 location pages not indexed; service pages get 104 sessions and 0 conversions in 365 days because the interlinking SOP routes equity to homepage + utility pages and bypasses services entirely. Backlinks carry a spam score of 43 from PR placements to PBN-adjacent domains (national benchmarks sit at 16–24). Q2 move: disavow first, then ship homepage CRD + 28 location-page redirects + 9 location-page rebuilds + service-page rebuild starting with alcohol-detox + opioid-detox + MAT, refactor internal linking onto services, sequence GBP optimization beyond reviews into categories/attributes/photos/posts/Q&A/booking link, and run individual review on 555 legacy URLs the topical+backlinks overlay flagged as AUDIT. Pause new PR placement. Lean on GBP dominance as the resilience layer.

kpis.label: Maps anchor — `alcohol rehab brooklyn`
kpis.detail: Avg rank 1.47 / 96% top-3 across the 136-cell LD grid at Coney Island center (Apr 2026). Improving 1.88 → 1.47 over 5 scans (Oct 2025 → Apr 2026). Rank #1 at 4 of 7 target anchor coords (Coney Island, Bath Beach, Bensonhurst, Brighton Beach) on both alcohol-rehab and drug-rehab queries.

kpis.label: GBP conversion rate vs Organic
kpis.detail: GMB-tagged sessions (`source=gmb`, isolated by custom segment in GA4) convert at 2.53% (1,186 sessions, 30 conv / 90d) vs all-Organic-Search at 0.32% (55 conv / 17,285 sess). Note: the GBP listing URL carries `utm_medium=click`, which GA4 default channel grouping doesn't recognize as Organic — these sessions land in Direct/Unassigned. The 2.53% is from a custom segment. Tracking caveats apply — see footer.

kpis.label: Review base velocity
kpis.detail: 91 / 4.5★ → 120 / 4.8★ in 16 days (Apr 22 → May 8). +29 reviews. 90d velocity ~5.2× the next-nearest direct Brooklyn competitor (ACI Inpatient: 5 reviews/90d). Only Brooklyn listing combining 4.8★ with 100+ reviews.

kpis.label: GSC daily impressions trend (87d)
kpis.detail: Feb 7 → May 4 2026 — daily impressions 101,622 → 21,220 peak-to-current. Total URLs known to Google −37% (2,097 → 1,313). 756 URLs flagged Crawled/Discovered — not indexed. Largest single explanatory piece: `/blog/how-to-get-rid-of-alcohol-breath` (230K imp / 90d, trend −85.9%, ~1.4M lost imp in window).

kpis.label: Service pages — 365d conv
kpis.detail: All 5 service pages combined: 104 sessions, 0 directly-attributed conversions, 365d. No on-page form; 0 schema; 0 OASAS/CARF/SAMHSA refs; 0 Coney Island mentions; 643–863 body words. Measurement + traffic story — interlinking SOP routed 305 link-plan rows to locations, 423 to blog, 15 to services.

kpis.label: Backlinks spam score + indexation
kpis.detail: Spam score 43 across 942 backlinks / 386 ref main domains — ~2× the national-brand benchmark (AAC 16, Recovery Village 24). Source: PR placements to PBN-adjacent clusters (seo-anomaly-*, seo-cartel-*, bhs-links-*.xyz, australianwebdirectory.*). 99% of backlinks point to homepage. 47 of 99 location pages not indexed; 37 of 99 are 33–37-word empty templates.

trend.headline: GA4 — sessions + conversions by channel, 18 months. Conversion events (90d): phone (116) + form_submit (58) + email (7)
trend.annotation_text: GA4 default-channel-grouping. Conversions = phone + form_submit + email events flagged as conversion. Tracking gaps: `gmb_click` (584/90d) and `contact_form_submit` (61/90d) are tracked as events but NOT marked as conversions in GA4; `form_submit` fires 237×/90d but only 58 are flagged (~24%). Numbers below understate true lead volume — GA4 admin fixes pending.
trend.may_extrapolation_note: May 2026 partial — first ~20 days of the month at trailing daily rate.
trend.quarterly_notes: gbp_sess = GMB-source-tagged sessions per month (source=gmb in GA4). gbp_conv only populated for the current quarter where we have the 90d KPI (30 conv / 1,186 sess / 2.53%). Historical GBP-attributed conversions aren't broken out in the underlying GA4 export.

levers.name: Site architecture cleanup — execution layer
levers.why: Google has already imposed the cleanup decision — 756 URLs flagged Crawled/Discovered — not indexed across 87 days. The architecture is what drags every downstream rebuild into a discounted site-level quality denominator. The topical+backlinks overlay rescued 453 URLs from auto-removal into AUDIT; the per-URL evidence model replaces auto-delete. All 270 mechanical operations ship in one M1 deploy window alongside a sitemap rebuild — spreading across weeks creates contradictory intermediate states.

actions.name: Location pages cleanup: 28 redirects (5 NJ + 23 NY-area → /areas-served) + write content for 9 upgrade pages (brooklyn, manhattan, NYC, queens, staten-island, bronx, harlem, inwood, brownsville)
Deploy LocalBusiness + BreadcrumbList schema on the 9 upgrade pages
NOINDEX execution on the 128 audited URLs (topical+backlinks overlay pre-filtered from 447) — includes 7-URL high-traffic wrong-audience cohort (breath / viagra / rappers / phobias)
AUDIT triage M1 batch: 75 of 555 AUDIT URLs (core-topical blogs first) — rewrite + internal-link to relevant service OR NOINDEX/REDIRECT
DELETE+REDIRECT: 99 thin/orphan via HTTP 410 + 43 doorways via 301 → /areas-served or /treatments/*
AUDIT triage continues — process remaining ~480 URLs (core-topical + unclassified-w/-traffic + framework-ambiguous)
DELETE 99 thin/orphan blog posts (down from 223 in v1 — topical overlay preserved 124 core-topical)
Indexation re-run on 47 not-indexed location URLs + Request Indexing for 9 LP-01 upgrades
Quarterly NOINDEX + AUDIT re-audit — recheck ranking recovery, backlinks, conversions; re-bucket on evidence

levers.outcome: Indexed surface 878 → ~665 URLs (waste removed; AUDIT lane preserved for triage). Daily impressions bottom within 30–45 days post-deploy → recovery slope as remaining surface re-evaluated as cleaner. Site-level quality denominator shifts from addiction-treatment + breath/phobias/rappers/NJ doorways to a focused addiction-treatment surface.

levers.name: Homepage CRD + service-page rebuild + interlinking refactor
levers.why: Homepage carries 67% of 833 annual conversions; 15 named core/utility pages carry another 31%. Service pages = 0%. Interlinking SOP gave 305 link-plan rows to locations, 423 to blog, 15 to services across the audit period. The rebuild + refactor moves the surface that already converts (1.0% Direct rate) onto every service line. Stalled 2 months on owner sign-off — build now, deploy on approval.

actions.name: Homepage build — 24 CRD items (H-01 to H-24) + 9 beyond-CRD competitive additions (service-area map embed, named clinician block, OASAS/CARF/Medicaid panels, neighborhood enumeration)
Meta + title refresh on top 5 conversion pages — homepage, /admissions, /contact-us, /treatments, /areas-served (meta is owner-unblocked)
/treatments hub upgrade — 16 items (T-01 to T-16): Service schema, H2 anchor IDs, insurance section, clinical-team section, FAQ, reviews block, named MAT clinician
Build /treatments/alcohol-detox — Wave 1 sub-page (largest aligned cluster, 4,583 kw vol; SP wins maps at 1.47)
Build /treatments/drug-detox — broad-coverage sub-page (drug cluster: 3 kw, SP #1 in pack across all 3)
/services/* → /treatments/* legacy 301 batch — 5 redirects (alcohol-detox, opioid-detox-mat, drug-detox, benzo-detox, comprehensive-short-term)
Internal linking refactor — every retained blog + retained location page links to the relevant service page (~3–5 contextual links per source); build locations × services matrix
Build /treatments/opioid-detox-mat — MAT-specific sub-page (closes LLM-citation gap on MAT-opioid)
Build /treatments/dual-diagnosis — CBT-grounded sub-page (KD 2.9, lowest in entire kw universe; closes LLM-citation gap on dual-diagnosis-brooklyn)
Sitewide schema audit + gap fix (non-/locations/) — Service on each /treatments/*, Article on top 20 blogs, BreadcrumbList sitewide

levers.outcome: Service-page sessions 104 → 500+ per quarter on alcohol-detox + opioid-detox-mat. Service-page internal-link count: 0 → 50+ each. Homepage avg position 27.4 → top 10 branded. /treatments 30-day post-deploy CTR ≥ 1% (from 0.2%).

levers.name: GBP optimization beyond reviews — depth + audit-risk closure
levers.why: GBP is the working channel — 2.53% conv vs 0.32% all-organic (8×). Listing is winning despite nine field-level leaks. Three audit-driven URGENT items the Q2 doc didn't catalog: 0% owner-response across 120 reviews including a 20-wk-old RN-flagged HIPAA-adjacent 5-star, 20-neighborhood service-area on an inpatient-only facility (down-weights distance algorithm), 'Entrance on W 24th St' embedded in address line breaks NAP-dedup at Yelp/Foursquare/BBB/Apple Maps.

actions.name: Backfill owner responses to 4 one-stars + 2 compliance-flagged 5-stars (Jacob Gaik RN, Pedro) — template-driven, brand-appropriate, no admission of liability
Address line cleanup — move 'Entrance on West 24th Street' out of street line; propagate clean NAP to 8–10 priority citations

Service area decision — remove (Path A) or trim to 9 South-Brooklyn neighborhoods (Path B). Current 20-area sprawl includes 7 out-of-borough (Howard Beach / Belle Harbor / Rockaway Park) on an inpatient-only facility
GBP Posts cadence start — 1/week minimum, intake-driven topics (insurance, MAT, neighborhood, CARF)
Q&A audit — owner-answer all 4 existing unanswered Q's + seed 10–15 patient-and-family-facing Q&A pairs with full answers (vs Urban 15, ACI 10)
Review-velocity push — intake-discharge SOP refresh; +20 reviews / M1 cadence (120 → 140+)
GBP services list audit — populate Dual Diagnosis Treatment (top-3 priority), fix 'Benzodiazepines' → 'Benzodiazepine'; per-secondary-category service lists (4 of 5 cats currently empty)
Attribute completion — verify + add `service_options:has_onsite_services`, `accessibility:has_wheelchair_accessible_parking`, `amenities:has_restroom_unisex` (5 → 8)
Category expansion — add Counselor (defensible) + verify Psychiatrist/Psychologist/Psychotherapist with staff credentials before claim (Ascendant uses all 4)
Description rewrite — replace generic 471-char with 633-char OASAS-licensed + CARF Center of Excellence + MAT meds + dual diagnosis + Medicaid/Aetna/Cigna/BCBS + Coney Island
GBP book-online URL — add Calendly/HubSpot intake; UNIQUE-WIN — no competitor in 11-pin set has this configured
Photo blitz — +25 photos (9 → 30+, Mountainside benchmark); cover photo first, mix of exterior/interior/staff/documentation/neighborhood
Posts cadence M2 — 2/week; ≥2 link to /treatments/alcohol-detox, ≥1 link to /treatments/drug-detox
Posts cadence M3 + Services list audit — confirm all 5 clinical services + per-secondary-cat completions
Owner replies to all <4-star reviews + sustain 20+/month velocity protocol (48h response window)

levers.outcome: Audit-risk content (Jacob Gaik HIPAA + Nick Tonjuk theft) closed within 1 week. Profile depth reaches Ascendant/Mountainside benchmark on photos / categories / attributes / Q&A. Booking-link CTA appears in Knowledge Panel — uncontested in competitor set. Map-pack grid (1.47 / 96% top-3) protected as resilience layer; does not need to move; needs to not regress.

levers.name: Citations + backlinks — disavow first, then opportunity
levers.why: Backlinks spam score 43 = ~2× national benchmarks. Source: three programmatic networks (seo-anomaly-* 106 domains, seo-cartel-* 40, bhs-links-*.xyz 5) plus Australian-PBN ring + one-off high-spam. GSC disavow processing takes 2–4 weeks — submitting first means the toxic drag clears while citation acquisition ramps. Competitor-gap analysis (Ascendant + ACI + Genesis, HQDM peers stripped) produced 385 domains → deduped 738 inputs across 3 sources → 697 unique → 684 open opportunities.

actions.name: Disavow toxic PBN inbound via GSC — 183 AUTO-disavow + 42 borderline manual review (full backlinks/live pull + spam-pattern regex)
Foundational citations Phase 1 — Psychology Today, SAMHSA Treatment Locator, NYS OASAS Find a Provider, BBB, Apple Maps, Bing Places, Foursquare, Healthgrades, Yelp claim/optimize
Pause new PR placements on the existing 10-domain target list pending citation-strategy review (Aaron Gruenke consultation pending per Milica 2026-05-13)
Press release on CARF Center of Excellence designation — anchor Coney Island + Brooklyn + CARF + addiction treatment; distribute regional health-trade outlets
Citation submissions batch 2 — insurance-directory tier (Aetna / Cigna / BCBS / Medicaid + 1 national)
Citations batch 3 — recovery-vertical authority tier (best-rehabs.com, sobasearch.com, alcoholism.org, helpguide.org + 2 Brooklyn-local health resources)
Earned-mention outreach — 3–5 real Brooklyn/NYC publications (NY1, Brooklyn Magazine, local NPR, real health/recovery editorial); pitch named-clinician bylines
Disavow re-pull — diff vs M1; add new spam clusters; re-upload updated disavow file
Backlinks re-pull at Q3 — measure spam-score trend post-disavow (target 43 → <30)

levers.outcome: Spam score trending 43 → <30 by Q3. 8–10 foundational citations live by M1 (also unlocks Gemini LLM citation surface). 5–10 topical-local citations live by M2. 1–2 quality earned mentions / D-A backlinks by M3.

levers.name: Content pipeline redirect — pause volume, refresh bleeders, service-aligned seeds
levers.why: 147 of 208 blog posts in bleeding cohort (−50% to −85% trend / 90d). Largest 5 pieces account for >3M lost impressions in 90 days. Asana pipeline allocated 19% of 1,294 tasks to blog and only 0.8% to service pages. Pivot is task-mix, not capacity. Suburb-page cadence (3/month) stays inside target radius — no more out-of-borough, no NJ.

actions.name: Suburb pages M1 batch — 3 inside Coney Island radius (Mermaid Avenue / Sea Gate strip / West Brighton beach strip)
Blog refresh M1 — 3 on-topic bleeding posts (largest of the 147-bleeder cohort) per HQDM refresh SOP
Suburb pages M2 batch — Bath Beach / Bensonhurst / Bay Ridge axis; cross-link to /treatments/alcohol-detox + /areas-served
Treatments support article batch — 3 articles building internal-link gravity to SP-01/SP-02 (e.g., What to expect in alcohol detox brooklyn / MAT vs traditional detox / How long does detox take)
Blog refresh M2 — 3 on-topic bleeding posts; track 30-day rank-recovery delta
PAA pipeline redirect — switch topic seeds from generic to Surfpoint-service-aligned (demand-gap + LLM research-phase gaps); 1–2 high-quality pieces ship from existing infrastructure
Suburb pages M3 batch — outer-ring (Sunset Park / Sheepshead Bay / Marine Park); cross-link to SP-03/SP-04 new sub-pages
LLM-format Q&A article batch — 3 PAA + AI Overview targeting (Is there a Suboxone clinic in Coney Island? / Best dual-diagnosis program in Brooklyn / Medicaid for opioid detox)
Blog refresh M3 — 3 on-topic bleeding posts; complete 9-post Q2 refresh cycle
Quality blog production (1–2/month) using `choosing-best-rehab-in-brooklyn` template; topic selection from demand-gap + LLM research-phase gap prompts

levers.outcome: Top-5 bleeders refreshed; 30-day rank-recovery delta logged. Suburb-page footprint expanded inside catchment (9 net-new). PAA pipeline pivoted to service-aligned. First research-phase LLM citations begin to appear by Q3.

levers.name: LLM visibility + reporting + cross-account coordination
levers.why: Surfpoint cited on 7 of 18 prompts (39%) and is the #1 cited rehab provider domain (15 citations vs Urban Recovery's 13). Strong on commercial-local Brooklyn; gap on 3 service-line prompts (dual diagnosis, MAT-opioid, short-term inpatient) and all 8 research-phase prompts. Gemini cites 0 (leans on government directories — M1-14 citation push unlocks this). LD coverage is only 1 keyword today; cannot measure Δ on the other 4 service clusters without baselining scans now.

actions.name: LD scan expansion — 8 priority terms × 2 coords = 16 grids (drug rehab brooklyn / alcohol rehab brooklyn / detox brooklyn / opioid rehab brooklyn / dual diagnosis brooklyn / MAT clinic brooklyn / addiction treatment brooklyn / suboxone clinic brooklyn at Coney Island + Brighton Beach). Weekly cadence
LegitScript verification via public registry + homepage source inspection — compliance status confirmed; gating dependency for any future paid acquisition
LLM citation gap closure — targeted on-page + schema on dual-diagnosis, MAT-opioid, short-term-rehab service pages; re-audit end of M2 targeting cited on 9/10 commercial-local prompts
LLM visibility re-audit (scripts/llm_visibility_surfpoint.py operational) — target 9/10 commercial-local + 2/8 research-phase cite rate
Expand LD coverage — add opioid/MAT, dual diagnosis, drug detox brooklyn keywords (no LD tracking today per §1.3)

Surfpoint vs Urban Recovery + Niagara Recovery internal coordination — agreed territory split or coordinated optimization across the 6 HQDM Recovery accounts (e.g., Urban owns Red Hook + Sunset Park, Surfpoint owns Coney Island + Bay Ridge)

Q2 close-out — planned → executed → impact correlation report (Marija Reporting Master Sheet — Result/Notes + Evidence columns populated; LD scan deltas, indexation tracker, review-count delta, GBP Posts CTR)

levers.outcome: 16 LD grids baselined by M1; 4-week trend by M3. LLM cite rate on commercial-local: 7/10 → 9/10 by M3. Cross-account coordination with Milica agreed before Q3. First HQDM strategy-first report ships under Phase 1 correlation criteria.

deliverables.label: Task1 M1 Location Cleanup Brief (LP-01 detail)
Location Pages Uniqueness (40 verbose-slug Q3 batch)

owner_asks: Publish the homepage CRD update — drafted March 2026, in client review 2 months. Either approve as-is or tell us 2–3 changes; we ship within 48h. Every downstream improvement (Treatments hub, sub-pages, schema) inherits homepage structure.

Authorize removal/redirect of 28 empty location pages — 37 of 99 are 33–37-word boilerplate doorways; 47/99 not indexed; 5 are NJ out-of-state on a NY-only OASAS-licensed facility. Decisions: 9 build-out + 28 redirect → /areas-served or parent location pages.
Authorize the 5 /services/* → /treatments/* legacy 301 redirects — orphaned service pages need URL canonicalization before SP-01..SP-04 ship.
URL normalization for 40 verbose-slug location pages — conceptual buy-in only; execution Phase 2 (Q3 2026) when ready (40 redirects, mapped 1:1, single batch).

Confirm review-acquisition SOP can run unattended at intake-discharge — QR card + 24h SMS — without weekly nudging. Velocity has moved 91 → 120 in 16 days; we want to formalize so the +20/month cadence continues.

Photo content for GBP catalog — share 25+ photos via Drive (interior / exterior / staff with releases / facility detail / Coney Island neighborhood context). Cover photo is highest priority; SP currently at 9 photos vs Mountainside 30 / Ascendant 90. We geotag and upload.
Disavow file upload — informational, not blocking. HQDM uploads the 183 AUTO-disavow + 42 borderline file to GSC via existing Search Console access. Likely contributing to the −79% impression drop.

gsc_organic_trend.headline: Daily impressions −79% peak-to-current (Feb 7 → May 4 2026). Crawl-budget re-evaluation March 16 (524 requests vs 70–100 baseline) — algorithmic action recognizing low-quality patterns at scale.
gsc_organic_trend.annotation_text: Source: GSC Search Analytics — daily 365d export aggregated to month. May 2026 partial (first 8 days). The drop is blog-cohort (147 of 208 blog posts in bleeding cohort, −50% to −85% trend / 90d); homepage and the 16 core pages (which carry 98.5% of conversions) impressions remain stable.
gsc_organic_trend.may_extrapolation_note: May 2026 partial — first 8 days at trailing pace.

snapshot.eyebrow: HQDM Search Intelligence · Prepared by Aleksandar Spasevski
snapshot.title: Surfpoint Recovery
snapshot.subtitle: Q2 2026 · Coney Island · South Brooklyn · Client Snapshot
snapshot.footer: Client snapshot · Q2 2026 · Prepared by Aleksandar Spasevski
snapshot.exec_html: Surfpoint is punching above its website. Your Google Business listing is the standout asset — average Maps rank 1.47 with 96% top-3 coverage on the core alcohol-rehab search, a 4.8★ / 120-review profile (up 29 reviews in 16 days), and Google-Business clicks convert at 2.53% — roughly 8× the rest of organic. The website is the bottleneck: search impressions are down 79% as architecture issues and over-built pages drag. The plan cleans up the site, rebuilds the homepage and service pages, and protects the Google Business lead.

quarters.title: By Quarter · Organic visitors + GBP clicks

snapshot.boxes_reading_html: Organic traffic and Maps visibility are the clean signals this quarter. YoY compares the most recent complete quarter (Q1 2026) against the same quarter prior year — the current Q2 is partial. Conversion volume is understated by known GA4 tracking gaps (`gmb_click` 584/90d + `contact_form_submit` 61/90d not flagged as conversions), so absolute conversion counts are excluded from these comparisons.

calendar.title: 3-Month Delivery Calendar
calendar.subtitle: Jun → Aug 2026 · exact pages + on-page actions per month. Cadence per HQDM scope: 1–2 services + 5 blogs + 3 suburbs / month, plus on-page per need (redirects, internal links, GBP wiring). maps.title: Maps Movement · Top Keywords
maps.window: Grid trend (Oct '25 → Apr '26) + anchor recheck (May 8 → May 28 '26)
maps.note_html: Row 1 = LocalDominator grid trend (avg rank across 136 ranked cells of the 169-cell 13×13 grid, 5 scans Oct 2025 → Apr 2026). Rows 2–6 = DataForSEO Maps Pack at Coney Island anchor, May 8 → May 28 2026. Only positive / hold movements shown — `drug rehab near me` slipped 1 → 3 at Coney over the window and is excluded; site-decline context aside, the priority service-line ranks held.

competitors.title: Competitor Movement · Maps — Losing Ground
competitors.window: Oct 2025 → Apr 2026 · LD grid delta (`alcohol rehab brooklyn` anchor)
competitors.note_html: Brooklyn-grid losers vs Oct 2025 baseline — every cell they vacated is grid space Surfpoint can absorb. Biggest mover: Teen Challenge (132 → 11 cells, −121). ACI Inpatient is the meaningful direct competitor here — keeping the grid coverage but bleeding −24 top-3 cells. Surfpoint avg rank improved from 1.88 → 1.47 (+0.41) across the same 6-month window.
